rationale: The citing paper uses Anderson and Simester (2013) as a key prior result on positive competitive advertising spillovers, specifically that competitor catalogs can increase focal-brand sales. It then positions its own contribution as going beyond that single-medium setting to estimate own- and cross-brand effects across multiple media, brands, and purchase channels. The paper also returns to this citation in the results/discussion to interpret similar positive catalog spillovers and to contrast boundary conditions (e.g., why their study finds both positive and negative cross-effects). This is more than a passing mention because it informs framing, empirical positioning, and interpretation of findings.
relation_description: The 2020 study extends Anderson and Simester (2013) from a direct-mail competitor spillover setting to a broader multimedia, multibrand, multichannel framework. It uses the 2013 finding as a benchmark and then identifies when cross-brand spillovers are positive versus negative under richer market and media conditions.
